results.title: Graphite: The AI-powered pull request toolchain | Product Hunt
results.content:
Graphite ReviewerYour high-signal AI code review companion

Launched on October 1st, 2024

Graphite

GraphiteHow the fastest developers ship code

Launched on September 19th, 2023

## Forum Threads

Graphitep/graphiteMerrill Lutsky•

1yr ago

### You're doing code reviews wrong - AMA w/ CEO of Graphite

THIS AMA WILL GO LIVE AT 9am PST March 19  
Hey Product Hunt!

I m Merrill Lutsky, co-founder & CEO of Graphite, the code review platform for the age of AI.

Graphitep/graphiteChris Messina•

6mo ago

### Graphite is being acquired by Cursor [...] •29 reviews

Our engineering team leans on Graphite Reviewer for pull request reviews. It doesn’t replace a human reviewer, but will catch a few things a week that our human reviewers miss. And the Graphite reviewer usually runs before a human reviewer gets to the pull request. Which is nice for saving expensive, human time. On an engineering team, for every hour of code writing, there are 10 hours of code fixing and maintenance. Graphite is pretty key for us to reducing that fixing and maintenance time. Another nifty thing about Graphite is pull request stacking. Our engineering team spans 14 timezones at Product Hunt. It’s not unusual for someone to crank out a bunch of work while the rest of the team is asleep. Pull request stacking is quite helpful for smoothing out that workflow. [...] Graphite is joining Cursor

View all

4.9

Based on 42 reviews

Review Graphite?

Reviewers mostly praise Graphite for making stacked pull requests practical on GitHub, helping teams break large changes into smaller reviews, stay unblocked, and move faster with more confidence. Users repeatedly highlight the ergonomic CLI, easy GitHub setup, auto-restacking and rebasing, and a polished UI and keyboard-driven review flow. A few also say it works well even for ordinary PRs, not just stacks. The main caveats are a somewhat steep learning curve and some dislike of the diff view, especially the comment panel layout.

+39

results.title: Artificial Intelligence | Product Hunt
results.content:
Image 111: ProdShort ProdShort

Is every product suddenly becoming an “AI agent”?
I was reading this recent @OpenAI article about Gartner naming OpenAI a Leader in enterprise AI coding agents: 

Image 112
"Software development is becoming more agentic." This is a good summary of what is happening right now. We are moving from AI that helps you write faster, to AI that can take over tasks (actions, use tools, make changes, run tests, and bring the work back for human review). That is a very different behavior. 

The article gives Cisco as an example. They used Codex for a big part of their AI Defense platform and reduced delivery time from several quarters to a few weeks.

21 comments•333 views

1mo ago

results.title: lablab.ai Reviews (2026) | Product Hunt
results.content:
3.7

Based on 3 reviews

Review lablab.ai?

Lablab.ai is praised for its vibrant community and well-organized hackathons, which inspire makers to create innovative products like StudyRecon. Many users appreciate the platform's ability to foster collaboration and skill development in AI, highlighting its user-friendly design and supportive environment. However, some users have experienced issues with the registration and submission processes, citing delays and inefficiencies. Overall, Lablab.ai is seen as a valuable resource for AI enthusiasts looking to innovate and connect with like-minded individuals.

I have been a member since it was 1k users and I was fascinated by the process and how strong is the community to boost and empower AI builders, highly recommending LabLab for whomever want to make an impact using AI

#### What's great

strong community (2)large user base (1)

Report

 4 views [...] 2 views

•1 review

I found Lablab.ai to be a really disorganized platform. After registering, the approval process took over a month, despite my repeated messages to the admin. The communication was not helpful, making the whole experience quite frustrating. Additionally, the submission process is bizarre and inefficient. I tried to submit entries for several hackathons, but it never worked out, which was incredibly disappointing. Overall, I haven't had a positive experience with this platform, and I would hesitate to recommend it to others.

#### What needs improvement

poor communication (1)disorganized platform (1)inefficient submission process (1)
